# WebSoroban docs

> Write, compile, deploy, and call Soroban smart contracts from the browser — no local toolchain.

## Getting started

- [Overview](/docs/getting-started): What WebSoroban is, how signing in provisions your testnet wallet, and the edit-compile-deploy-invoke loop.
- [Quickstart](/docs/getting-started/quickstart): Deploy a counter contract to testnet and call it — start to finish in about a minute.

## Core concepts

- [Core concepts](/docs/core-concepts): Soroban and testnet in plain terms, the per-user wallet model, projects vs files, and what each action does.

## The IDE

- [The IDE](/docs/ide): The three-zone layout, the file tree, multi-file projects and module resolution, the command palette, and autosave.

## Compile

- [Compile](/docs/compile): Build your crate to a wasm32v1-none binary, read per-file diagnostics, and fix pre-flight validation errors.

## Deploy

- [Deploy](/docs/deploy): Upload your Wasm and instantiate a contract on testnet, signed with your per-user wallet.

## Invoke

- [Invoke](/docs/invoke): Call contract functions with typed inputs — the full ScType entry reference, read vs signed writes, and how to read results.

## Test

- [Test](/docs/test): Run cargo test in the IDE, read per-file results, and replay saved function tests by simulation.

## Templates

- [Templates](/docs/templates): Scaffold a complete contract project from a verified bundle that builds and tests out of the box.
- [Contract wizard](/docs/templates/wizard): Customise a token, NFT, or counter with feature toggles and a live preview, then scaffold a compile-ready project.

## Wallets & faucet

- [Wallets & faucet](/docs/wallets): Your per-user testnet wallet — where it comes from, what the browser sees, and how to refund it from the faucet.

## Plans & limits

- [Plans and limits](/docs/plans-and-limits): What each plan allows for deploys and function tests, how the monthly counters reset, and what happens when you hit a cap.

## Troubleshooting

- [Troubleshooting](/docs/troubleshooting): The errors you hit compiling, deploying, and invoking on WebSoroban — what each message means and the fix.

## Reference

- [Glossary](/docs/reference): The Soroban and WebSoroban terms used across these docs, defined precisely.
- [API reference](/docs/reference/api): The public WebSoroban backend endpoints — request bodies, responses, and which ones sign with your wallet.
